0.title: Code of Ethical and Business Conduct
0.content: Texagon aims to create and maintain an environment that is ethical and respectful towards all employees and business operations. We expect all organization members to comply with the rules and regulations and take responsibility for their actions. This Code of Ethical and Business Conduct applies to all stakeholders and members of the company regardless of gender, age, language, caste, ethnicity, religion, cultural background, nationality, beliefs, disabilities, or any other form of physical or mental difference. This code is in place to help establish an acceptable and positive working environment for all organization members. All employees are responsible for upholding this code by ensuring that any observed injustice or unfair practice is reported immediately to the relevant authorities (management, human resources personnel, or any direct supervisor). This helps the company maintain a healthy and positive environment by involving all employees in the process. Employees are encouraged to report any information regarding violations of this code without fear of reprisal or retaliation. Employees who wish to remain anonymous can report such matters to HR confidentially. Any failure to comply with this code of conduct may lead to disciplinary actions, up to and including immediate termination of employment.

1.title: Code of Conduct
1.content: . | Ethical and Respectful Behavior: All employees are required to act and behave ethically and respectfully towards all other employees, customers, and company staff at all times. This includes maintaining their actions and words within respectable boundaries. Employees are expected to use kind words, maintain professional boundaries with colleagues, and show everyone equal respect. | Commitment to Company Interests: Texagon’s business ventures demand that employees act in the company’s best interest, both ethically and legally. | Non-Disruption Clause: Employees must agree that during and after their employment with Texagon, they will not attempt to disrupt, damage, or slander the company or its relationships with customers, members, employees, or external parties. | Dedication to Employment: Employees are required to give their complete commitment to Texagon during their employment. They may not engage in any activity that disrupts their work commitment or leads to non-compliance with this code of conduct. | Protection of Company Property: Employees must not engage in any act that damages the company’s tangible or intangible property. | Punctuality and Timeliness: Texagon values punctuality and expects employees to strictly adhere to prescribed work timings. Adjustments may occur based on business demands, such as during public or company events. | Harassment and Discrimination Prohibited: Any form of harassment or discrimination based on sex, age, caste, ethnic background, religious beliefs, sect, disability, medical condition, marital status, caregiving responsibilities, or other factors is strictly prohibited. This includes physical and verbal harassment in any form. Employees are encouraged to foster an inclusive environment where people from different backgrounds are treated equally and with respect. | Unacceptable Actions: While it is impossible to list every undesirable action, employees must use sensible judgment to avoid behaviors such as falsification, lying, defamation, omission of facts, disclosure of trade secrets, threatening others, verbal or physical abuse, long-term patterns of absenteeism or tardiness, possession of illegal items, and unauthorized access to documents or property. | .

2.title: Reporting and Enforcement
2.content: . | All employees must report observed violations of this code to the appropriate authority (management, HR, or direct supervisors). | Employees reporting violations will be protected from retaliation and may report anonymously if they prefer. | Violations of this code will be subject to strict investigation and disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. | .

3.title: Conclusion
3.content: Employment at Texagon is a result of mutual consent and should be taken seriously. Defiance or disregard of this code will result in appropriate disciplinary measures. Employees are expected to uphold this code to foster a respectful, ethical, and positive work environment for all.

4.title: Welcome to Texagon’s Employee Handbook
4.content: This handbook contains everything you need to know about working at Texagon. Use the navigation sidebar to explore the sections.

7.title: Leave Management
7.content: We trust our team to act responsibly and be accountable for their actions. Leaves can be taken as needed, but they must be pre-planned, structured, and approved by the manager in advance, except in cases of emergencies. | Paid Leaves: . | Each full-time employee is entitled to 5 paid casual leaves and 8 paid sick leaves per calendar year. | The calendar year for all leave categories runs from January to December. | Casual leaves are accrued on a pro-rata basis, at 0.42 days per month (calculated as 5 days divided by 12 months). | Sick leaves are accrued on a pro-rata basis, at 0.67 days per month (calculated as 8 days divided by 12 months). | Leave entitlement is calculated based on the month and date of employment start within the given year. | Leaves do not carry over to the next year. | . | Leave Request Procedure: . | Every leave must be requested at least one week in advance; otherwise, it will not be approved. | Leave requests made just one or two days prior will not be accepted, except in emergencies. | Each team member must obtain approval from their reporting head, project manager, HR, and other relevant team or project members. | Leave requests should be submitted via email and approved by the designated individuals in the proper format. | . | Accrued Leaves: . | Leaves can only be taken from the earned balance at any given time, as they are accrued monthly. | If the requested leaves exceed the earned balance, approval will depend on project and team needs, as well as management’s discretion. Extra leaves may be taken as unpaid, depending on the business situation. | . | Casual Leave Approval: . | Approval must be obtained from the relevant supervisor or project manager before HR can give final approval. | Taking leave in advance from next month’s allotment is at HR’s discretion, based on individual performance and the current business situation. | . | . a. Casual Leaves . Each team member is entitled to 5 paid casual leaves per calendar year, to be used for emergencies or unforeseen circumstances. | Casual leaves are accrued on a pro-rata basis, meaning 0.42 days per month (calculated as 5 days divided by 12 months). | Casual leave requests must be submitted via email with at least one full day’s notice or before the workday begins. | For leave longer than one day, requests should be made at least two weeks in advance. | Proof of the emergency or circumstance is required to approve leave. | Approval is subject to the team’s needs and management’s discretion. | Leave requests made within a shorter notice period may not be approved unless deemed necessary by the manager or HR. | . b. Sick Leaves . Each team member is entitled to 8 paid sick leaves per calendar year. | Sick leaves are accrued on a pro-rata basis, meaning 0.67 days per month (calculated as 8 days divided by 12 months). | Sick leave requests must be submitted via email at least one full day before the leave or by 08:00 AM on the day of absence. | If three or more sick days are requested, a doctor’s note is required. | Failure to provide necessary documentation may result in unpaid leave or denial of the request. | All relevant team members, HR, and the team lead/supervisors must be informed of the situation. | Failure to follow this procedure may result in disciplinary actions, including deductions from leave balances, unpaid leave, or, in extreme cases, termination of employment due to repeated violations of the leave policy. | . c. Unpaid Leaves . | Unpaid leaves are allowed if business operations are not affected. These leaves require prior approval from the management. | Employers reserve the right to reject leave applications if absence would negatively impact business operations. | . Note . These guidelines, policies, and procedures are designed to ensure consistency and fairness in all processes, ensuring that every active team member is treated equally in accordance with Texagon’s established policies. At Texagon, we are committed to maintaining a workplace free from discrimination, while implementing an efficient workflow system. If any team member has a specific or unique query, they are encouraged to reach out to the HR representative for assistance. This document has been approved and published by Texagon’s management and may be updated or modified as needed, at the discretion of the management.

11.title: Purpose
11.content: Texagon is committed to continuous improvement and professional growth. The Team Evaluation Policy ensures that every project concludes with a structured assessment of team performance, fostering a culture of learning and excellence.

12.title: Scope
12.content: This policy applies to all project teams at Texagon. Evaluations are conducted by the Team Lead and Project Manager upon project completion.

13.title: Evaluation Process
13.content: . | Timing: Evaluations occur at the end of each project before final closure. | Participants: The Team Lead and Project Manager separately assess team performance. | Method: A standardized evaluation document is completed for each team. | .

14.title: Evaluation Criteria
14.content: The evaluation document will include the following key sections: . 1. Strengths . | Identify key skills and competencies demonstrated by the team. | Highlight instances of effective collaboration, problem-solving, and technical excellence. | Acknowledge outstanding contributions by individuals and the team as a whole. | . 2. Areas for Improvement . | Identify gaps in skills, knowledge, or processes that affected the project. | Highlight specific challenges that impacted performance and suggest ways to overcome them. | Address any recurring issues that require attention for future projects. | . 3. Suggestions for Growth . | Recommend training, workshops, or mentorship programs to improve skills. | Suggest process improvements for better efficiency and productivity. | Encourage knowledge-sharing initiatives within the team. | . 4. General Notes . | Capture additional observations not covered in the above sections. | Include feedback on project-specific challenges, client interactions, or technical hurdles. | Provide any relevant insights that could benefit future projects. | .

15.title: Review & Action Plan
15.content: . | The completed evaluation document is reviewed by senior management. | Actionable insights are used to refine training programs and project workflows. | Teams are encouraged to discuss feedback in a constructive manner. | .

16.title: Impact on Employee Growth
16.content: . | The evaluation results will be used to determine increments, bonuses, promotions, and long-term career growth within the company. | Performance assessments will influence an employee’s future at Texagon, ensuring fair recognition and reward for contributions. | Constructive feedback will help employees align with the company’s expectations and professional development goals. | .

17.title: Confidentiality & Transparency
17.content: . | Evaluation documents are confidential and accessible only to relevant stakeholders. | Feedback should be objective, constructive, and aimed at team growth. | . Texagon is committed to fostering a positive and productive work environment through structured evaluations that drive continuous improvement. This policy ensures that every project contributes to the professional development of our teams and the overall success of our company.

20.title: Purpose
20.content: This policy establishes clear working hours and ensures adequate provisions for lunch and prayer breaks during office hours to accommodate employees’ needs. Policy Effective Date: 1st February 2025 .

21.title: Office Hours
21.content: . | Regular Working Hours: 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM | Total Work Duration: 9 hours (including breaks) | .

22.title: Break Schedule
22.content: To ensure productivity and accommodate personal needs, the following breaks will be observed: . Lunch Break . | Duration: 1 hour | Timing: 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM | . Prayer Breaks . | Zuhr Prayer Break: . | Duration: 15 minutes | Timing: Around 1:15 PM to 1:30 PM (adjustable based on prayer time) | . | Asar Prayer Break: . | Duration: 15 minutes | Timing: Around 4:15 PM to 4:30 PM (adjustable based on prayer time) | . | Maghrib Prayer Break: . | Duration: 10 minutes | Timing: Around sunset, approximately 6:00 PM to 6:10 PM (adjustable based on prayer time) | . | .

23.title: Break Guidelines
23.content: . | Adherence: Employees are encouraged to adhere strictly to the allocated times for each break. | Prayer Breaks: Designed to provide sufficient time for employees to perform prayers without interrupting workflow. | Adjustments: The exact timing of prayer breaks may be adjusted during the year to align with varying prayer times. | Supervisors’ Role: Supervisors are responsible for ensuring that the break schedule does not affect business operations. | .

24.title: Implementation
24.content: . | Employees should coordinate with their teams to ensure proper coverage during prayer and lunch breaks. | Managers are encouraged to support employees in utilizing these breaks appropriately. | .

25.title: Flexibility
25.content: . | Special Periods: Flexibility in break timings may be provided during specific periods (e.g., Ramadan) to accommodate longer prayer times or fasting needs. | Adjustments: Managers can make changes to the break schedule and must communicate these adjustments in advance to employees. | .

26.title: Compliance
26.content: . | All employees are expected to comply with the break timings to maintain a balance between personal and professional responsibilities. | Managers must ensure the policy is implemented fairly and equitably across all teams. | Managers, Leads, PMs, and the COO have the discretion to modify break timings as necessary. | .

27.title: Additional Notes
27.content: . | Prayer Time Alignment: Adjustments to prayer break timings will be made throughout the year to align with the exact prayer times. | Queries: If any team member has specific or unique queries, they are encouraged to reach out to the HR representative for assistance. | . This document has been approved and published by Texagon’s management. Updates or modifications may be made as needed, at the discretion of the management.
